title: China's Jan-May battery exports reach $40bn as new markets emerge
gist: China's lithium-ion battery exports hit $8B in May 2026 (up 37% YoY), bringing the Jan-May total to $40B (up 45% vs 2025) and 145GWh. A record $9.7B March was likely front-loaded ahead of the 1 April battery export rebate cut, after which volumes normalized but stayed well above 2025 levels.

insights.claim: China's lithium-ion battery exports reached US$8 billion in May 2026, up 37% year-on-year, bringing the year-to-May total to $40 billion, a 45% increase over the same 2025 period.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Confirms China's dominant and accelerating battery export position; bullish for Chinese cell makers, watch trade/tariff response.
insights.quote: China's lithium ion battery exports reached US$8 billion in May 2026, up 37% year-on-year (YOY), bringing the year to May total to $40 billion.
insights.locator: China's lithium ion battery exports reached US$8 billion

insights.claim: Per CABIA data, China's battery exports reached 29GWh in May 2026, taking the Jan-May total to 145GWh.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Volume metric alongside value confirms real capacity throughput, not just price effects.
insights.quote: exports reached 29GWh in May, taking the Jan–May total to 145GWh.
insights.locator: According to China Automotive Battery Innovation Alliance

insights.claim: China's March 2026 battery exports hit a record $9.7 billion, likely driven by exporters pulling forward shipments ahead of the 1 April cut to the battery export rebate, with April and May normalizing but staying well above 2025.
insights.speaker: Benchmark Team
insights.so_what: Policy-driven front-loading distorts the quarter; underlying trend remains strong, but watch for payback in coming months.
insights.quote: March exports hit a record $9.7 billion, likely driven in part by exporters pulling forward shipments ahead of the 1 April cut to the battery export rebate.
insights.locator: The year to May figures reflect a front-loaded quarter.
